steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The logical relationship is specification. Sentence 1 makes a broad claim — Downs draws on "a variety of Mexican musical genres." The blank needs a transition signaling that sentence 2 gives concrete examples of those genres.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Specifically": signals that what follows narrows down to concrete particulars of the general claim, introducing corridos, rancheras, and other northern genres as the specific examples, so fits. ✓
  • Choice B — "Therefore": signals cause-effect, implying drawing on corridos and rancheras is a logical consequence of using a variety of genres. Sentence 2 is not a consequence — it is an elaboration. ✗
  • Choice C — "In conclusion": signals a final summary of prior points. Sentence 2 is an elaboration, not a concluding statement. ✗
  • Choice D — "Rather": signals contrast or correction. Sentence 2 does not contradict or replace sentence 1's claim — it illustrates it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
